channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Man says social security benefits terminated without warning or explanation
item.description: A local retiree says his Social Security benefits were suddenly suspended without warning — and with no explanation given when he reached out. He worries it may have to do with the place he was born, and ongoing DOGE cutbacks.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Humphrey/KFOR, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Most disapprove of Trump's approach to Ukraine, Russia: Survey
item.description: Nearly six in 10 people, 59 percent, said it was “not too" or “not at all likely” that Trump’s approach to the Russia-Ukraine war will bring long-term peace between the two Eastern European countries. Around 41 percent disagreed.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Rubio says South Africa’s ambassador to the US 'is no longer welcome' in the country
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Secretary of State Marco Rubio said Friday that South Africa’s ambassador to the United States “is no longer welcome” in the country.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Bill to avert government shutdown clears key hurdle with help from Democrats
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A spending bill to avert a partial government shutdown narrowly cleared a key procedural hurdle in the Senate, paving the way for passage as a midnight Friday deadline loomed. Ten Democrats joined with Republicans to clear the 60-vote threshold needed to advance the measure.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING and LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump environmental rollbacks would boost pollution and endanger lives, former EPA heads say
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Three former Environmental Protection Agency leaders sounded an alarm Friday, saying rollbacks proposed by EPA Administrator Lee Zeldin endanger the lives of millions of Americans and abandon the agency's dual mission to protect the environment and human health.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump pledges to 'expel rogue actors' in political speech at Justice Department
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump pledged to “expose” his enemies during a norm-breaking political speech at the Justice Department in which he aired a litany of grievances against the criminal cases he faced and vowed retribution for what he described as the “lies and abuses that have occurr
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Viral 'American Idol' contestant dead at 32, family says
item.description: Douglas Kiker, the garbage collector who touched hearts with his singing voice on "American Idol" has died, his family announced on social media. He was 32 years old.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: Illinois votes on a new state flag design
item.description: SPRINGFIELD, Ill. (AP) — Flags engender such loyalty and pride, they often become very personal emblems with quirky or quaint nicknames: Old Glory, Union Jack, Maple Leaf — or SOB.
item.dc:creator: JOHN O'CONNOR, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Viral 'American Idol' contestant dead at 32, family says
item.description: Douglas Kiker, the garbage collector who touched hearts with his singing voice on "American Idol" has died, his family announced on social media. He was 32 years old.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KTLA 5

item.title: Federal judge considers blocking DOGE from accessing Social Security data of millions of Americans
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — A federal judge is considering whether to temporarily block Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency from accessing Social Security Administration systems that hold sensitive data on millions of Americans.
item.dc:creator: LEA SKENE and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Helicopters permanently restricted on route near Washington airport where 67 died in midair crash
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Helicopters will be permanently restricted from flying near Washington, D.C.'s airport on the same route where a passenger jet and an Army helicopter collided in midair, killing 67 people, the Federal Aviation Administration said Friday.
item.dc:creator: JOHN SEEWER and TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mace sued for defamation over allegations in stunning floor speech
item.description: A man Rep. Nancy Mace (R-S.C.) accused of being part of a group of “predators” in a stunning House floor speech is suing her for defamation.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Boy, 13, critically injured while subway surfing in New York
item.description: He was taken to NYC Health + Hospitals/Elmhurst, police said. An investigation into the incident is ongoing, according to authorities.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, NewsNation

item.title: Illegal immigrant with multiple DWI, theft convictions deported again: ICE
item.description: A Mexican woman with a long criminal history who was previously removed from the U.S. four times was deported again on Thursday, immigration authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cause of death for Gene Hackman and Betsy Arakawa's dog released
item.description: An examination of the dog found dead along with actor Gene Hackman and his wife in their Santa Fe home shows dehydration and starvation were likely what led to the animal’s demise.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Big March storm system threatens US with tornadoes, blizzards and wildfire risk
item.description: A huge storm system crossing the U.S. threatens to unleash tornadoes Friday in the Mississippi Valley, blizzards in the northern Plains and dry, gusty conditions in Texas and Oklahoma that pose a high risk of wildfires.
item.dc:creator: RUSS BYNUM Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Protestant denominations try new ideas as they face declines in members and money
item.description: When the Episcopal Church recently announced cuts to its national staff, it was the latest in a long-running cycle among historic U.S. Protestant denominations — declines in members leading to declines in funding and thus in staff. And it wasn't alone. The Presbyterian Church (U.S.A.
item.dc:creator: PETER SMITH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Key witness in American college student's disappearance tells police how they met, what happened on beach
item.description: Key witness in missing American college student Sudiksha Konanki's disappearance in the Dominican Republic says they were hit by a big wave before she vanished.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hundreds of federal offices may start shutting down this summer due to DOGE
item.description: Federal agencies will begin to vacate hundreds of offices across the country this summer under a frenetic and error-riddled push by Elon Musk’s budget-cutting advisers to terminate leases that they say waste money.
item.dc:creator: By RYAN J. FOLEY, JOSHUA GOODMAN and CHRISTOPHER L. KELLER Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Trump's tariff wars forge rare bipartisan alliance in Kentucky as bourbon makers fear escalation
item.description: FRANKFORT, Ky. (AP) — President Donald Trump's blunt-force use of tariffs has forged a rare bipartisan alliance among Kentucky's top leaders — Democratic Gov. Andy Beshear and Republican Sens. Mitch McConnell and Rand Paul — in lamenting brewing trade wars that could stagger the bourbon sector.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: More than 50 universities face federal investigations as part of Trump's anti-DEI campaign
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — More than 50 universities are being investigated for alleged racial discrimination as part of President Donald Trump’s campaign to end diversity, equity and inclusion programs that his officials say exclude white and Asian American students.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Newborn kittens rescued from wrecked car moments before demolition
item.description: A litter of kittens was rescued from the inside of a wrecked car at a junkyard moments before it was scheduled to be demolished Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Euzarraga, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Former US Sen. Alan Simpson dies at age 93
item.description: Former U.S. Sen. Alan Simpson, a political legend whose quick wit bridged partisan gaps in the years before today’s political acrimony, has died. He was 93.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Spring break travelers urged to take certain precautions amid search for missing American college student
item.description: Former U.S. ambassador to the Dominican Republic Robin Bernstein, who served between 2018 and 2021, has a warning for spring breakers in light of the disappearance of Sudiksha Konanki.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Once-routine immigration check-ins become high-stakes calculation as some are detained
item.description: When a couple from Colombia who was planning their wedding showed up for a check-in with U.S. immigration authorities, one was given his next appointment date. The other was detained and deported. Jhojan doesn’t know why Felipe was detained at the Feb.
item.dc:creator: VALERIE GONZALEZ, CEDAR ATTANASIO and SOPHIA TAREEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge orders Trump to rehire probationary workers let go in mass firings across multiple agencies
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — A federal judge in San Francisco ordered President Donald Trump's administration to rehire thousands, if not tens of thousands, of probationary workers let go in mass firings across multiple agencies, blasting their tactics Thursday as he slowed the new president's dramatic down
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Connecticut woman arrested after stepson allegedly held captive for over 20 years
item.description: A Connecticut woman accused of kidnapping a man and holding him captive for over 20 years was arrested Wednesday, police said.
item.dc:creator: Juliana Lepore, WFLA, NBC

item.title: FAFSA site goes down after mass layoff at Education Department
item.description: The Studentaid.gov website was down for hours on Wednesday after the Education Department laid off half its workforce.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WGN 9

item.title: Explosion at Texas Tech causes evacuations, immediate spring break
item.description: An explosion at a substation on the Texas Tech campus has caused power outages at the Texas Tech and the Health Sciences Center Wednesday evening, according to a TechAlert.
item.dc:creator: Georgina Johnston, WGN 9

item.title: 5 risks facing the US economy under Trump
item.description: Warning signs have been flashing and markets have been sliding amid policy uncertainties and some troubling macroeconomic indicators.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, WFLA, NBC

item.title: A government program made tax filing free and more efficient. Musk and DOGE may get rid of it anyway
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Mia Francis, a 22-year-old barista from Boston, filed her taxes on her own this year for the first time, using a free government tax filing program that made it easy because it did most of the work for her.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 4-year-old calls 911 to say 'bad mom' ate his ice cream and needed to go to jail
item.description: We've all been there. You go to the freezer and discover your ice cream has been eaten, and not by you.
item.dc:creator: Laura Morrison, Fox 5

item.title: What to know about US student Sudiksha Konanki, who vanished in Dominican Republic on spring break
item.description: SANTO DOMINGO, Dominican Republic (AP) — Investigators continue to search for a college student from Virginia who vanished earlier this month while visiting the Dominican Republic with five other people during spring break from classes.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Affordable housing threatened as Trump halts $1 billion slated for extending life of aging buildings
item.description: The Trump administration is halting a $1 billion program that helps preserve affordable housing, threatening projects that keep tens of thousands of units livable for low-income Americans, according to a document obtained by The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump administration drops lawsuit against company over alleged abuse at its child migrant shelters
item.description: McALLEN, Texas (AP) — The Trump administration moved to drop a civil lawsuit Wednesday against the largest provider of housing for migrant children over allegations of sexual abuse and harassment of unaccompanied minors, saying it also would no longer be using that company's services.
item.dc:creator: VALERIE GONZALEZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Missing woman survives 6 days trapped in wrecked car as drivers pass unaware
item.description: For six days, Brieonna Cassell remained trapped inside her mangled Taurus as hundreds of cars passed by, unaware someone was in the vehicle.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump to increase steel and aluminum tariffs on Canada to 50 percent in response to electricity surcharge
item.description: The uncertainty around Trump's tariff plans have led to a downturn in the stock market, and the president has not ruled out the possibility of a recession this year.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5
